bullets.details: Researchers have developed 'The AI Scientist,' an end-to-end system capable of generating scientific papers and passing peer review at top-tier machine learning workshops, highlighting massive potential for AI-led research at a fraction of human costs.
bullets.topic: AI Scientist Successfully Passes Academic Peer Review

bullets.details: Physical Intelligence, an AI robotics startup founded by former DeepMind staff, is reportedly in talks to raise a $1 billion funding round that would value the company at over $11 billion.
The Colorado House has passed HB 26-1210, a significant bill aiming to ban companies from using opaque consumer data and algorithms to enforce personalized pricing or wage setting.
Cursor has implemented real-time reinforcement learning for its Composer agent, updating the model every five hours based on live production data to bridge the train-test gap.
bullets.topic: Cursor Integrates Real-Time Reinforcement Learning for Coding Agents

bullets.details: OpenBSD has rejected an ext4 filesystem driver contribution after the author admitted it was generated entirely by LLMs, citing significant copyright and legal maintenance risks.
bullets.topic: OpenBSD Rejects LLM-Authored Code Over Copyright Ambiguity

bullets.details: New data reveals China has overtaken the U.S. in both total clinical drug trial volume and domestic-only research, underscoring a significant global shift in pharmaceutical R&D dominance.
bullets.topic: China Surpasses U.S. in Clinical Drug Trial Volume

bullets.details: Pro-Iranian hacking group Handala leaked older, non-government emails from FBI Director Kash Patel's personal account; while official data was not compromised, the breach highlights targeted digital operations.
bullets.topic: Iranian Hackers Leak FBI Director's Personal Emails

digest_summary: This week in tech, AI's role in research continues to expand, with a new system passing academic peer review while OpenBSD sets boundaries by rejecting LLM-generated code over legal risks. Meanwhile, Physical Intelligence nears a massive $11 billion valuation as AI robotics gains momentum, and Colorado takes legislative action to ban AI-driven surveillance pricing. Elsewhere, China has overtaken the U.S. in clinical drug trial volume, signaling a shift in global pharmaceutical R&D.
digest_title: AI Research Breakthroughs, Massive Robotics Funding, and New Regulatory Hurdles
